mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer credit outstanding and new housing starts indicates robust household balance sheet expansion and durable goods demand, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample tests, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate forward return regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings denominator for equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate cash flows materialize and analyst estimates are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDP growth exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and stronger supply-side momentum, which improves corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings surprises materialize.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation with SPX returns remains below 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above the Federal Reserve's implicit target forces prolonged monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs, compresses equity valuations, and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite sustained inflation above 3.0%, or the interaction between inflation and policy rates shows no incremental explanatory power for forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and stagnant real wage growth signals labor market overheating without corresponding consumer purchasing power expansion, which eventually triggers profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: The divergence signal consistently predicts positive returns, or the joint effect of employment and inflation growth fails to show statistical significance in a forward regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production relative to capacity constraints indicate efficient supply chain utilization and lower input cost inflation, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative when industrial production growth exceeds 0.5%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.
